terms.definition: Automated test exercising two or more real components together to verify their wiring and contracts; sits between unit and E2E in the test pyramid.
Test against one real external dependency at a time (e.g. real Postgres, everything else mocked).
Test against several real dependencies wired together (real DB + real cache + real queue).
Test verifying that a consumer's expectations of a provider's API match the provider's actual contract (e.g. Pact, OpenAPI mock servers).
A small live test against real external dependencies that verifies basic end-to-end functionality; often run nightly or on release.
Library (Java/Python/etc.) for spinning up real services (Postgres, Redis, Kafka) inside Docker containers per test run.
Recorded HTTP interaction (request + response) stored on disk and replayed by libraries like `vcrpy` for deterministic, free testing of HTTP-dependent code.
Short-lived stack (often per-PR) spun up in cloud / k8s for integration testing or preview, torn down after use.
Pytest decorator like `@pytest.mark.integration` used to group tests; combined with `-m` filters to run subsets.
